identity.name: Prisma Browser — Screen Capture of Sensitive SaaS Page (T1113)
identity.summary: A user programmatically screenshots a sanctioned CRM detail page tagged sensitive. Prisma Browser's screen-capture policy blocks/alerts on the capture against a sensitive-tagged origin, increments a screen-capture-attempt counter, and repeated captures from the same user drive an elevated user risk score that triggers a downstream conditional-access response in XSIAM.
identity.description: Screen capture defeats traditional DLP because no text or file ever leaves the page — the data is exfiltrated as pixels. Prisma Browser closes this by enforcing screen-capture policy against origins tagged sensitive: a `page.screenshot()` (or OS capture surface) against the sanctioned CRM detail page (`crm.cortexsim-test.invalid/account/AC-12345`, `#account-detail`) is blocked and logged, with each attempt incrementing a per-user counter. The cortex-browser-attacker EAL plugin drives navigate → screenshot → screenshot (two captures), emitting `prisma_browser_events` with `action=screen-capture`, `origin`, `data_classification`, `out_path`, and a running `screen_capture_attempt_count`. Repeated captures elevate the user's risk score, which XSIAM consumes to trigger a downstream conditional-access policy. Detection layers: a BIOC on screen capture against a sensitive-tagged origin; analytics on the attempt counter under sensitive-content policy; analytics on repeat captures contributing to the risk score; and an XSIAM analytic on the elevated risk score driving conditional access. A correlation rule fuses the screen-capture BIOC with the repeat-capture analytic into one collection incident.

prerequisites.description: Managed Prisma Browser with screen-capture policy enabled on sensitive-tagged origins; reporting telemetry to XSIAM.
prerequisites.check_command: python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli describe browser_attack_runner | jq -e '.eal_targets | length > 0'

prerequisites.description: Lab environment matching scenario SIM-BROWSER-005 (cortex-browser-attacker installed; CRM origin tagged sensitive).

payload.code:
# Drives the managed Prisma Browser through the screen-capture campaign.
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/sim-browser-005-eal.yml --live
# Campaign: navigate crm.../account/AC-12345 ; screenshot /tmp/browser-005-cap.png
#           screenshot /tmp/browser-005-cap-2.png (repeat -> risk-score elevation)

expected_artifacts.description: Screen-capture action against an origin tagged sensitive.
Repeat screen-capture from the same user incrementing the attempt counter.

biocs.name: Prisma Browser — page.screenshot() called against origin tagged sensitive — block + alert
biocs.description: Prisma Browser — page.screenshot() called against origin tagged sensitive — block + alert. A programmatic screen capture targets a page whose origin is classified sensitive; policy blocks and alerts.
biocs.logic:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter action = "screen-capture"
| filter data_classification in ("sensitive", "confidential", "restricted")
| filter origin in ("crm.cortexsim-test.invalid", "crm.corp.example.com", "salesforce.com")
| filter capture_method in ("page.screenshot", "getDisplayMedia", "os-capture")

xql_queries.name: Pre-flight: plugin metadata visible to operator
xql_queries.query:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter campaign_id = "BC-BROWSER-005"
| filter action in ("navigate", "screen-capture")
| fields _time, campaign_id, action, origin, url, out_path
| sort asc _time

xql_queries.name: Prisma Browser — screen-capture-attempt counter increments under sensitive-content policy
xql_queries.query:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter action = "screen-capture"
| filter data_classification in ("sensitive", "confidential", "restricted")
| comp count() as capture_attempts by user, origin
| filter capture_attempts >= 1
| fields user, origin, capture_attempts

xql_queries.name: Prisma Browser — repeat screen-capture from same user counts toward elevated risk score
xql_queries.query:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter action = "screen-capture"
| comp count() as captures by user
| filter captures >= 2
| fields user, captures

xql_queries.name: XSIAM — user risk score elevated, conditional-access policy triggered downstream
xql_queries.query:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter action = "screen-capture"
| comp count() as captures, max(user_risk_score) as risk_score by user
| filter captures >= 2 or risk_score >= 60
| fields user, captures, risk_score

correlation_rules.name: Browser Sensitive Screen Capture — Repeat Collection
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(Prisma Browser — page.screenshot() called against origin tagged sensitive — block + alert)

analytics_modules: Prisma Browser — Screen-capture policy on sensitive origins
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — User risk scoring + conditional-access trigger

products.evidence_query: dataset = prisma_browser_events | filter action = "screen-capture" and data_classification = "sensitive" | fields _time, user, origin
Correlation CR-BROWSER-0005 raises a collection incident; repeat captures elevate the user risk score and trigger conditional access.

use_cases.name: Browser Screen-Capture Policy Enforcement
use_cases.description: Block and detect screen captures of sensitive-tagged SaaS pages in the managed Prisma Browser, and escalate repeat offenders via user risk scoring.

test_cases.objective: Screen capture of a sensitive-tagged origin fires the Prisma Browser BIOC.
preconditions: Managed Prisma Browser with screen-capture policy enabled.
CRM origin tagged sensitive in policy.
steps: Observe the screen-capture alert in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: BIOC `Prisma Browser — page.screenshot() called against origin tagged sensitive — block + alert` fires within 60s.
Attempt-counter and repeat-capture XQL queries return >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Repeat captures elevate user risk score and trigger conditional access.
success_criteria: User risk-score XQL returns >= 1 row; downstream conditional-access policy is triggered.

preventive_controls: Enable Prisma Browser screen-capture policy in Block mode on origins tagged sensitive/confidential.
Apply watermarking and capture-deny to high-value SaaS detail pages.
Feed repeat screen-capture attempts into user risk scoring to drive step-up auth / conditional access.
detection_engineering: Alert on any screen-capture action against an origin classified sensitive.
Track per-user screen-capture attempt counts and escalate on repeat offenders.
Correlate elevated user risk score with the originating capture events for investigation context.
